title: LeCun’s “Superhuman Adaptable Intelligence” Is a Special Case of AGI, Not an Alternative
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > ... just sayin' ...
Content:
[](https://substack.com/@bengoertzel)
[Ben Goertzel](https://substack.com/@bengoertzel)
Mar 07, 2026
23
9 3
Share
Yann LeCun, together with Judah Goldfeder, Philippe Wyder, and Ravid Shwartz-Ziv, recently put out [a paper proposing](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.23643) **[Superhuman Adaptable Intelligence (SAI)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.23643)** [](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.23643) as a replacement for AGI as the north star of AI research. The paper is professionally done, of course, and has some interesting aspects.
Foundationally, though, one of the core dimensions of their proposal is just not correct. SAI is not a different thing from AGI, it’s just the form AGI takes if you impose some quite specific assumptions on what’s interesting to you and what isn’t.
